question.text: What is the minimum market maker inventory requirement at the time of allotment in an SME issue?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The market maker must hold a minimum inventory of 5% of the specified securities at the time of allotment. This ensures the market maker has sufficient stock to provide two-way quotes from day one of listing. **The Trap:** 15% is the minimum underwriting obligation of the lead manager on its own account, not the market maker inventory requirement. 10% is a common wrong answer that candidates confuse with other percentage thresholds. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the 5% inventory requirement alongside the 15% underwriting requirement — they are related but distinct obligations in SME issues. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 5% inventory is your market-making ammunition — if the stock opens weak, you need enough shares to provide meaningful buy-side liquidity.
